turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#050816"/>
  <g fill="#cfe3ff" opacity="0.9">
    <circle cx="85" cy="110" r="2"/>
    <circle cx="140" cy="75" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="210" cy="120" r="1.8"/>
    <circle cx="305" cy="85" r="1.7"/>
    <circle cx="380" cy="135" r="2.1"/>
    <circle cx="440" cy="90" r="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="70" cy="240" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="110" cy="300" r="2.0"/>
    <circle cx="165" cy="410" r="1.7"/>
    <circle cx="260" cy="440" r="2.2"/>
    <circle cx="360" cy="370" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="470" cy="300" r="1.8"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="420" r="1.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Saturn rings (behind) -->
  <g transform="translate(256 260) rotate(-18)" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="180" ry="60" stroke="#7f97ff" stroke-width="18" opacity="0.35"/>
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="190" ry="65" stroke="#9fb3ff" stroke-width="10" opacity="0.75"/>
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="160" ry="52" stroke="#bcd0ff" stroke-width="8" opacity="0.65"/>
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="120" ry="40" stroke="#9fb3ff" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.35"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Saturn planet -->
  <g transform="translate(256 270)">
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="95" fill="#c8a36a"/>
    <circle cx="-18" cy="-10" r="55" fill="#b88f55" opacity="0.55"/>
    <circle cx="18" cy="10" r="60" fill="#d3b27d" opacity="0.35"/>

    <!-- Cloud bands -->
    <g fill="none" stroke="#9d6f3b" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.45">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="-25" rx="82" ry="35"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="86" ry="37"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="25" rx="78" ry="33"/>
    </g>
    <g fill="none" stroke="#e7c48c" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.5">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="-12" rx="92" ry="40"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="14" rx="90" ry="39"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Rings (front) -->
    <g transform="rotate(-18)" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="5" rx="190" ry="65" stroke="#e7eefc" stroke-width="14" opacity="0.35"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="5" rx="175" ry="58" stroke="#c9d8ff" stroke-width="10" opacity="0.75"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="5" rx="150" ry="50" stroke="#7f97ff" stroke-width="7" opacity="0.55"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="5" rx="120" ry="40" stroke="#bcd0ff" stroke-width="5" opacity="0.4"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Light vignette -->
  <radialGradient id="glow" cx="50%" cy="52%" r="65%">
    <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#2a3d8f" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
    <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#050816" stop-opacity="0.0"/>
    <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#050816" stop-opacity="0.0"/>
  </radialGradient>
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#glow)"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating Saturn depiction**

I need to create a simple pictorial depiction of Saturn without any text. It should feature a ringed planet, so I plan to draw a circle for the planet and include two rings around it. A background with stars would add variety and depth to the image. I’ll output this in JSON format to ensure it's structured correctly for whatever purpose the user needs.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
